summary: Record follow-up hygiene from active external-report reconciliation and no-allowlist strict preview expansion.

observations.problem: Strict preview now audits the full public Python scope instead of a narrow allowlist, but the current full-scope preview still reports lint and strict mypy findings, so making that preview blocking immediately would break the normal static lane.
observations.why_it_matters: The no-allowlist direction is correct for long-term coverage, but promotion safety depends on separating full-scope diagnosis from blocking gates until the remaining findings are burned down or explicitly retired.
observations.automation_candidate: Use the strict lint/type uplift reports as the standing inventory for incremental cleanup, then promote strict-preview-audit from diagnostic to blocking only after it is clean on the full scope.
observations.suggested_followup: Planned follow-up: burn down the full-scope strict preview findings tracked by ops/reports/lint-uplift-plan.json and ops/reports/type-uplift-plan.json; keep the target full-scope and avoid reintroducing allowlists.
